summary: Fixed video playback and PDF access issues. Root cause: /uploads/ path was being handled by frontend (React) instead of backend. Solution: Changed static file mount from /uploads to /api/uploads in backend, and updated frontend getMediaUrl() to convert URLs to /api/uploads/ path.
critical_code_review_comments: Video file is 730MB .mov format - large file takes time to buffer. Consider converting to MP4 for better browser compatibility and smaller file size.
Old URLs stored in database still have wrong preview hostname - getMediaUrl() handles conversion but ideally URLs should be stored as relative paths only
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py - Changed static file mount from /uploads to /api/uploads, updated upload endpoints to return /api/uploads/ URLs
/app/frontend/src/App.js - Updated getMediaUrl() to convert /uploads/ paths to /api/uploads/
context_for_next_testing_agent: Video and PDF files are now accessible via /api/uploads/ path. The getMediaUrl() helper function in frontend converts old URLs with wrong preview hostnames to correct URLs. Video file is 730MB .mov which takes time to load - this is expected behavior, not a bug.

fixes_applied.issue: Video playback not working - FFmpegDemuxer error
fixes_applied.root_cause: /uploads/ path was being served by frontend (React) returning HTML instead of video file
fixes_applied.fix: Changed backend static file mount from /uploads to /api/uploads, updated frontend getMediaUrl() to use /api/uploads/ path

fixes_applied.root_cause: Same as video - /uploads/ path not routed to backend

verified_features: Admin panel accessible with all tabs (Dashboard, Courses, Book/PDF, Signals, News, Users)
Book saving in admin panel - title, description, PDF URL, cover URL, price all save correctly
Video URL correctly converted to /api/uploads/ path
PDF URL correctly converted to /api/uploads/ path
Book page shows 'You own this book' for admin
Read Book button opens correct PDF URL
Course modal shows video player with correct URL
